Solanum dulcamara, also known as Bittersweet Nightshade or Sicklera, is a perennial vine native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Solanum dulcamara include solanine, dulcin, and other glycoalkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
